skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-banana-pro</name>
    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

agent:main:main.lastHeartbeatText:
**✅ Option A Complete: Direct Spawn (2 Handoffs)**

**Architecture Simplified:**

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| 6 handoffs | **2 handoffs** |
| 4 intermediate files | **1 JSON queue** |
| 3 scripts | **1 script** |
| Complex debugging | **Simple queue inspection** |

**New Flow:**
```
Task → temujin/tasks/ → Heartbeat (15min) → spawn-pending.json → 
handle-spawns.py → sessions_spawn → Agent
```

**Files:**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `handle-spawns.py` | Reads queue, outputs spawns, auto-cleanup |
| `spawn-pending.json` | Single source of truth for pending spawns |

**Test Results:**
- ✅ Heartbeat pickup: 2 tasks queued
- ✅ Spawn handler: Processed both
- ✅ Agent 1: `temujin-direct-spawn-1` spawned
- ✅ Agent 2: `temujin-direct-spawn-2` spawned

**Cron Job:** Runs every 2 minutes, calls `handle-spawns.py` and executes spawns

**Next:** Want me to increase heartbeat frequency to 1 minute with local LLM?

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>

    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments.     <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>

    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>

    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>

    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <name>eightctl</name>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Requires a bash tool that supports pty:true.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
